title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Policy Clinic: Exception Policy
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates exception policy increment in a repository engineering contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.
Balance detection value, feedback speed, noise, and maintenance cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Exception Policy
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Exception Policy contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.

content.text: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Exception Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository engineering contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A disabled rule needs no explanation if CI passes. This fails the Exception Policy contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Exception Policy case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Exception Policy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

stimulus.title: Exception Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A disabled rule needs no explanation if CI passes.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Exception Policy, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.

steps.title: Build Exception Policy evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-exception-policy-sxfcfi: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Exception Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-exception-policy-sxfcfi:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Exception Policy model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Exception Policy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.
